chapter_title.en: The Prayer of the Two Eids

text: 1480 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَا مِنْ عِيدٍ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ أَضْحًى وَ لاَ فِطْرٍ إِلاَّ وَ هُوَ يُجَدَّدُ فِيهِ لآِلِ مُحَمَّدٍ حُزْنٌ» قِيلَ وَ لِمَ ذَلِكَ قَالَ «لِأَنَّهُمْ يَرَوْنَ حَقَّهُمْ فِي يَدِ غَيْرِهِمْ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1480 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said: "There is no Eid for the Muslims, whether it is Eid al-Adha or Eid al-Fitr, except that it renews grief for the family of Muhammad."
He was asked: "Why is that?"
Imam (as) said: "Because they see their rightful position in the hands of others”.
